acceptance_targets: [ch07-booker-001] Pass only if the revised ending at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:257 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:279 contains an observable irreversible turn: either Tiana arrives and is told on-page, or Marcia/Delroy/Micah takes a concrete action or decision that changes what chapter_08 starts from. Fail if the chapter still ends with characters merely listening for the side gate.

[ch07-prose-001] Pass only if snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:261 is rewritten so Delroy's evasive warmth is conveyed without echoing the style-bible example's syntax, key nouns, or causal structure. A reader comparing the revised line to the calibration note should not be able to map them as near-paraphrases.

[ch07-craft-001] Pass only if the revised ending includes one on-page bodily reaction and one spoken reaction tied to the cited span, plus a visible change in the room caused by the news. Fail if the revision still relies on suspense alone or summarizes the impact without dramatizing it.

[FBR-001] Pass if the chapter 7 opening at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2547 identifies one unambiguous church location that matches the continuity sheet or clearly distinguishes itself from it in-text; fail if 'New Testament Revival' remains unexplained and a reader could still infer two conflicting church sites.

[FBR-007] Pass if the reaction at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2571 is expressed through this room's social and physical detail rather than 'went/gone still' language; fail if Pauline's shock still lands through the same generic freeze used elsewhere in the book.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if the revised ending at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:257 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:279 contains an observable irreversible turn: either Tiana arrives and is told on-page, or Marcia/Delroy/Micah takes a concrete action or decision that changes what chapter_08 starts from. Fail if the chapter still ends with characters merely listening for the side gate.
findings.problem: The apartment aftermath stops one beat before the chapter's hardest private consequence lands. After the church scene delivers the public humiliation, the second scene becomes setup for the next chapter rather than a completed movement: Marcia warns against false hope, Tiana is named as the real audience for the bad news, and the chapter ends with everyone waiting for her instead of forcing an irreversible new turn.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the ending in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:257 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:279 so the scene lands its own final turn. Best option: let Tiana enter and receive the loss on-page here. Alternate option: if chapter_08 must keep the arrival, replace the current waiting beat with a different irreversible action in this room at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:267 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:279 that materially changes household leverage before the cut.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:261 is rewritten so Delroy's evasive warmth is conveyed without echoing the style-bible example's syntax, key nouns, or causal structure. A reader comparing the revised line to the calibration note should not be able to map them as near-paraphrases.
findings.problem: Delroy's interior read leaks style-bible scaffolding into the chapter. 'He could make bad news hold still for a few minutes' is a near-copy of the non-copyable calibration language for his voice, which makes the sentence feel generated from notes rather than discovered in-scene.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the Delroy summary at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:261 with fresh scene-specific language grounded in this apartment, this marriage, and Marcia's current disgust. Do not reuse the existing 'bad news' framing or another variant of the calibration sentence; make his charm legible through a new image or action pattern native to this moment.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if the revised ending includes one on-page bodily reaction and one spoken reaction tied to the cited span, plus a visible change in the room caused by the news. Fail if the revision still relies on suspense alone or summarizes the impact without dramatizing it.
findings.problem: The chapter shows timidity at the exact place it has earned maximum pressure. Once Marcia says Tiana must hear 'the truth,' the prose retreats into suspense instead of rendering the daughter's first bodily and verbal response to the lost money. That cutoff softens the emotional force of a scene built around public shame becoming private household damage.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:263 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:279 so the aftermath stays in the room long enough to show the disclosure hitting Tiana or, if the scene must remain pre-arrival, to substitute a comparably embodied consequence rather than anticipation. Anchor the rewrite to concrete objects already present on these lines: the envelopes, the notebook, the mug, the gate, the handkerchief.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the chapter 7 opening at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2547 identifies one unambiguous church location that matches the continuity sheet or clearly distinguishes itself from it in-text; fail if 'New Testament Revival' remains unexplained and a reader could still infer two conflicting church sites.
findings.problem: Chapter 7 names the church site 'New Testament Revival,' but the continuity sheet canonically identifies the shelter/fellowship location as New Testament Baptist fellowship hall. Because later flood-displacement scenes route the family to 'New Testament,' this chapter seeds a factual location drift that makes the church geography read as two competing institutions.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the opening location setup at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2547 so the site name matches the continuity sheet's canonical 'New Testament Baptist fellowship hall,' or explicitly establish that this is a different church in the same network and give the reader a clear relation between them within the same paragraph.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the reaction at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2571 is expressed through this room's social and physical detail rather than 'went/gone still' language; fail if Pauline's shock still lands through the same generic freeze used elsewhere in the book.
findings.problem: The novel's recurring 'went/gone/very still' tic appears in 10 chapters; here, Pauline 'went still' at the voice note, which flattens the fracture in the susu circle into a stock pause. This instance should be REPLACED, not kept.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2571, rewrite Pauline's response through fellowship-hall specifics such as the phone's weight in her hand, the cough-drop tin, chair legs, Styrofoam cups, or the women's collective lean toward the speaker. Remove the 'went still' phrasing itself.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into embarrassment, erotic consequence, moral compromise, and the ugly arithmetic of poverty. When a scene turns on prejudice, bureaucracy, violence, or family betrayal, stay with the room long enough for the reader to feel what each choice costs. Resolve edge cases by choosing the more specific and less flattering truth unless it breaks established character logic.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, racism, injury, debt, hunger, or shame is rendered abstractly, rewrite until the bodily detail, social pressure, and changed leverage are visible on the page.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high — contractions are the spoken norm across the novel; uncontracted forms should appear only for emphasis, ceremony, legal language, or character-specific pressure.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Vary scene count, dialogue density, and chapter motor across the book: pair public humiliation with private aftermath, follow argument chapters with information-turn or weather chapters, preserve at least three genuinely quiet domestic or communal stretches, and never let more than three chapters in a row hinge on debate followed by decisive action.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary and concrete, with Caribbean-American texture carried naturally in syntax and reference rather than in italicized glossary behavior; lyrical when earned, blunt when the room turns brutal.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person that rotates by chapter; intimate enough for bodily shame and private contradiction, but alert to class performance, racial reading, and room dynamics.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate lean pressure sentences with occasional longer image-bearing sentences; lyric lift should arrive at emotional turns, never as constant prestige haze.
